remember: Students will be able to recall the key milestones and events in the rise of popular movements in India.
Students will be able to identify the major leaders and their role in the popular movements.
understand: Students will be able to explain the causes and impact of popular movements on Indian society.
Students will be able to describe the characteristics and features of popular movements in India.
apply: Students will be able to analyze the similarities and differences between various popular movements in India.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of popular movements in bringing about social and political change in India.
analyze: Students will be able to critically evaluate the strengths and limitations of popular movements in India.
Students will be able to assess the long-term impact of popular movements on Indian society and politics.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, students will be able to understand the rise of popular movements in India, including the Non-Cooperation Movement and the Quit India Movement.
Students will be able to analyze the role of Mahatma Gandhi and other leaders in these movements.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of popular movements on Indian society and politics.
board_exam_objectives: Students should be able to explain the causes and effects of the Non-Cooperation Movement and the Quit India Movement.
Students should be able to describe the role of Mahatma Gandhi and other leaders in these movements.
Students should be able to analyze the significance of popular movements in Indian history and their impact on Indian society and politics.
